metadata.name: Bargain — Torch the Tower without and with bargain cost
metadata.description: Tests Torch the Tower's Bargain mechanic (CR 702.166) in two sequential scenarios using a shared initial state. Scenario 1: p1 casts Torch the Tower ({R}) WITHOUT bargaining, targeting p2's Scroll Thief (1/3). The spell deals 2 damage (the if_false branch of Condition::WasBargained). 2 damage on a 1/3 is not lethal — Scroll Thief survives with 2 damage marked. Scenario 2: p1 casts a second Torch the Tower WITH bargain, sacrificing a Sol Ring (artifact) as the optional additional cost (CR 702.166a). The sacrifice happens at cast time as part of paying the cost (CR 118.8). The spell is marked 'was bargained' on the stack. It targets p2's Bog Raiders (2/2) and deals 3 damage (the if_true branch). 3 damage meets or exceeds the 2 toughness of Bog Raiders — SBA 704.5g destroys it. Both scenarios confirm Condition::WasBargained branching in the card definition resolves correctly.
metadata.generation_notes:
Torch the Tower (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/torch_the_tower.rs): mana_cost {R}, Instant, Bargain. Card definition uses AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Bargain) and AbilityDefinition::Spell { effect: Effect::Conditional { condition: Condition::WasBargained, if_true: DealDamage(Fixed(3)), if_false: DealDamage(Fixed(2)) }, targets: [TargetRequirement::TargetPermanent], ... }. Note: the oracle text also says 'the controller can't gain life this turn' when bargained, but that half is not yet implemented in the card def (CantGainLife effect variant missing) — only the 3-damage branch is tested here.

Bargain (CR 702.166a): optional additional cost — sacrifice an artifact, enchantment, or token as you cast the spell. This is an additional cost (CR 118.8), not an alternative cost, so the normal mana cost {R} is still paid. The engine validates the sacrificed object at casting.rs:1394-1408: checks is_artifact (CardType::Artifact in card_types), is_enchantment (CardType::Enchantment), or is_token (obj.is_token). AltCostKind is NOT used for Bargain — bargain_sacrifice is a separate Option<ObjectId> field on Command::CastSpell.

Why Sol Ring as the bargain sacrifice: Tokens placed in the initial_state battlefield via the harness are built with make_spec() + enrich_spec_from_def(). Since 'Treasure' has no CardDefinition in the registry, enrich_spec_from_def is a no-op and the resulting object has empty card_types and is_token=false. The engine's bargain validation would therefore reject it. Sol Ring IS a registered CardDefinition (CardType::Artifact), so enrich_spec_from_def correctly populates card_types=[Artifact] and the bargain validation passes. Sol Ring is a legal bargain sacrifice (artifact).

Harness support: 'cast_spell_bargain' action type is implemented in replay_harness.rs translate_player_action(). The field 'bargain_sacrifice' in the JSON PlayerAction is deserialized as Option<String> (script_schema.rs:285) and resolved via find_on_battlefield(state, player, name) which matches obj.characteristics.name == name.

Scenario 1 target: Scroll Thief (1/3). 2 damage < 3 toughness — not lethal. No SBA needed.
Scenario 2 target: Bog Raiders (2/2). 3 damage >= 2 toughness — lethal, SBA 704.5g destroys it.

Mana: p1 starts with {red: 2} for both {R} casts.

script.step_note: Scenario 1: p1 casts Torch the Tower WITHOUT bargaining, targeting Scroll Thief (1/3). Deals 2 damage — not lethal, creature survives.

actions.description: Initial: p1 has 2x Torch the Tower in hand and Sol Ring on battlefield; p2 has Scroll Thief and Bog Raiders; both players at 40 life
actions.note: p1 has {R}{R} in pool. Sol Ring (artifact) is on p1's battlefield — will be used as bargain sacrifice in scenario 2. Scroll Thief (1/3) and Bog Raiders (2/2) are on p2's battlefield.

CR 601.2: p1 casts Torch the Tower ({R}) without paying the bargain cost. No sacrifice is made. bargain_sacrifice is absent (None) — Condition::WasBargained will be false at resolution. Target is Scroll Thief controlled by p2.

actions.description: After casting without bargain: Torch the Tower on stack; p1 hand down to 1 card; Sol Ring still on p1 battlefield
actions.note: No sacrifice was made — Sol Ring is still on p1's battlefield. The spell is on the stack with was_bargained=false.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack (Torch the Tower) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.166b + 120.3: Spell was NOT bargained (was_bargained=false), so Condition::WasBargained selects the if_false branch — deals 2 damage to Scroll Thief. 2 damage marked on Scroll Thief (1/3).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Torch the Tower resolves. Condition::WasBargained is false — the if_false branch (DealDamage Fixed(2)) executes. Spell moves to p1's graveyard.

actions.description: After unbargained resolution: Scroll Thief still alive (2 damage < 3 toughness); no SBA; Torch the Tower in p1 graveyard; Sol Ring still on p1 battlefield
actions.note: CR 120.3e: 2 damage is marked on Scroll Thief. Since 2 < 3 (toughness), the creature is not at lethal damage — SBA 704.5g does not trigger. Torch the Tower is in p1's graveyard. Sol Ring remains for use in scenario 2.

script.step_note: Scenario 2: p1 casts Torch the Tower WITH bargain, sacrificing Sol Ring (artifact) as the optional additional cost (CR 702.166a). Deals 3 damage to Bog Raiders (2/2) — lethal, SBA destroys it.

actions.description: Before bargained cast: p1 has 1 Torch the Tower in hand and Sol Ring on battlefield; Bog Raiders on p2 battlefield
actions.note: p1 still has {R} in pool. Sol Ring (artifact) is on battlefield, available to sacrifice as the bargain cost.

CR 702.166a: p1 pays the bargain cost by sacrificing Sol Ring (an artifact — satisfies CR 702.166a 'artifact, enchantment, or token') in addition to paying the normal mana cost {R}. CR 118.8: Bargain is an additional cost; the normal mana cost is still paid. Sol Ring is sacrificed at cast time. The stack object is created with was_bargained=true.

actions.description: After bargained cast: Torch the Tower on stack (was_bargained=true); Sol Ring sacrificed and gone from battlefield; p1 hand empty
actions.note: CR 702.166a: Sol Ring was sacrificed as the bargain cost at cast time — it is no longer on p1's battlefield. The spell is on the stack targeting Bog Raiders with was_bargained=true.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack (Torch the Tower) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.166b + 120.3: Spell was bargained (was_bargained=true), so Condition::WasBargained selects the if_true branch — deals 3 damage to Bog Raiders. 3 damage on a 2/2 meets or exceeds its toughness of 2.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Bargained Torch the Tower resolves. Condition::WasBargained is true — the if_true branch (DealDamage Fixed(3)) executes. Spell moves to p1's graveyard.
CR 704.5g: Bog Raiders (2/2) has 3 damage marked on it, which is >= its toughness of 2. SBA destroys it and moves it to p2's graveyard.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked after resolution. 3 damage >= 2 toughness — lethal.

actions.description: Final: Bog Raiders dead (in p2 graveyard); both Torch the Towers in p1 graveyard; Scroll Thief still alive on p2 battlefield; both players at 40 life
actions.note: Bargained Torch the Tower dealt 3 damage to Bog Raiders (2/2) — lethal by SBA. Scroll Thief survived scenario 1 (2 damage < 3 toughness) and remains on p2's battlefield. Both Torch the Towers are in p1's graveyard. Life totals unaffected — all damage targeted creatures. This confirms Condition::WasBargained correctly branches: unbargained=2 damage, bargained=3 damage.
